channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: As the National Guard enters Memphis, memories of MLK and 1968 unrest resurface
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — As National Guard troops arrive in Memphis, the memory of thousands of them with bayonetted rifles and tanks in 1968 is still fresh for Joe Calhoun. Back then, he marched in the streets with sanitation workers and the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: MIT rejects Trump admin funding compact, citing free expression concerns
item.description: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) rejected a Trump administration proposal that would usher in sweeping reforms in exchange for benefits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A sacred lake, a Columbus statue, and an Indigenous people's long struggle for land
item.description: GEDDES, N.Y. (AP) — Before white settlers came to Onondaga Lake, before the city of Syracuse grew along its shore, before the pristine waters became one of the most polluted bodies of water in the United States, it was a sacred place for the Onondaga Nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Passenger says Uber driver accused in Palisades Fire was ‘nut job’ who ranted about Trump
item.description: Passenger Brennan White describes Palisades Fire suspect Jonathan Rinderknecht as a "nut job" who bashed President Donald Trump during Uber ride.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans try to weaken 50-year-old law protecting whales, seals and polar bears
item.description: One of the U.S.’s longest standing pieces of environmental legislation, credited with helping save rare whales from extinction, is the subject of an effort for cutbacks from Republican lawmakers who now feel they have the political will to do so.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers seek dismissal of federal charges in assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Luigi Mangione asked a New York federal judge Saturday to dismiss some criminal charges, including the only count for which he could face the death penalty, from a federal indictment brought against him in the December assassination of UnitedHealthcare’s chief executive.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A food aid program that supports millions of low-income mothers and their young children received a $300 million infusion from the Trump administration this week, alleviating some anxiety that it would run out of money during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet your favorite streamers at TwitchCon San Diego 2025
item.description: Video game streamers will take over the San Diego Convention Center for a weekend of meet and greets, interactive workshops, live esports and more at TwitchCon San Diego 2025, set for Oct. 17-19.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Bodycam video: Sisters arrested for attacking gate agent at airport
item.description: Newly-released body camera video shows the moment two sisters were arrested for allegedly assaulting a Frontier Airlines gate agent.
item.dc:creator: Sara Kitchin, NewsNation

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend. While the storm affecting the eastern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, KRON4

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Ground stop issued at busy Atlanta airport following fire incident with 'strong gas fumes,' FAA confirms
item.description: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport experienced a ground stop Friday afternoon due to a fire incident involving "gas fumes," according to the FAA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cash App giving $100 to users through October. How to enter
item.description: CashApp is giving away $100 to thousands of users every Friday through Oct. 24, as part of its Cash App Pool sweepstakes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Critically endangered baby black rhino makes its debut at Cleveland zoo
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — A critically endangered rhino made its public debut Friday at a Cleveland zoo, dashing about its enclosure and giving a boost to the endangered species.
item.dc:creator: SUE OGROCKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House to begin 'substantial' shutdown layoffs of federal workers
item.description: The White House said Friday it had started laying off federal workers, following through on a threat to do so amid an ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: White House to begin 'substantial' shutdown layoffs of federal workers
item.description: The White House said Friday it had started laying off federal workers, following through on a threat to do so amid an ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: 22 states are in a recession or on the brink: Moody’s
item.description: It’s more sobering news for the economy as last week U.S. hiring plans fell to their lowest level since 2009.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Accused Charlie Kirk assassin wants to ditch jail clothes for upcoming court appearances
item.description: Tyler Robinson's attorneys argue that wearing civilian clothing during court appearances will help preserve his constitutional right to a fair trial in the Charlie Kirk murder case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man convicted of killing a 15-year-old girl in her home in 2001 is executed by injection in Indiana
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2001 rape and murder of a teenage girl was executed by injection early Friday in the state’s third execution since resuming capital punishment last year. Roy Lee Ward, 53, was put to death at the Indiana State Prison in Michigan City.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lyft scraps feature that let drivers see how often riders tip
item.description: Lyft has scrapped a test feature that showed some drivers how often passengers tip before accepting a ride.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump does not win Nobel Peace Prize despite Israel-Hamas deal and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taco Bell, KFC franchisee using AI to improve customer experience
item.description: Rather than displacing employees, as some fear AI might, Coach AI can serve as "a tool to help the worker.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Spanberger, Earle-Sears face off in Nexstar's Virginia gubernatorial debate
item.description: Former U.S. Congresswoman Abigail Spanberger and current Virginia Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears squared off Thursday evening in their only scheduled live debate in the race for Virginia governor.
item.dc:creator: Jane Alvarez-Wertz, NewsNation

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgment
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Antifa expert at Rutgers University says he is moving to Spain because of death threats
item.description: An expert on anti-fascist groups who teaches at Rutgers University says he is fleeing with his family to Spain after getting death threats amid a campaign by the campus chapter of Turning Point USA and other conservative groups to get him fired.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nobel Prize in literature awarded to Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai
item.description: Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai, whose philosophical, bleakly funny novels often unfold in single sentences, won the Nobel Prize in literature Thursday for his for his "compelling and visionary oeuvre that, in the midst of apocalyptic terror, reaffirms the power of art.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV and MIKE CORDER Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elton John, Olivia Rodrigo, Doja Cat, Questlove, Twenty One Pilots and Raye to present at Rock Hall
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A glittering list of music figures will help present awards at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame induction ceremony this year, including Elton John, Olivia Rodrigo, Doja Cat, Questlove, Twenty One Pilots, Missy Elliott and Raye.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5
